dsVertClient 1.1.0

Federated ordinal logistic regression (naive cumulative binomials)

Source: R/ds.vertOrdinal.R
ds.vertOrdinal.Rd

Fit a K-category ordinal logistic regression by training K-1 cumulative binomial logistics of the form \(P(Y \leq k | X) = \sigma(\theta_k - X^\top \beta)\), one for each threshold level. This is the NAIVE approach: each cumulative binomial is fit independently, giving per-level beta estimates that may differ across thresholds. Proper proportional-odds fitting with a single shared beta and K-1 threshold parameters jointly optimised requires a dedicated joint L-BFGS objective (Month 2 follow-on).

The naive version is useful for exploratory analyses and for testing the proportional-odds assumption by comparing the beta estimates across threshold levels.

Usage

ds.vertOrdinal(
  formula,
  data = NULL,
  levels_ordered,
  cumulative_template = "%s_leq",
  verbose = TRUE,
  datasources = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

formula

R formula with the ORDERED outcome on the LHS (passed through as a factor level name in the per-threshold formulas).

data

Name of the aligned data frame on each server.

levels_ordered

Character vector of the ordered levels, smallest-to-largest.

...

Passed to each underlying ds.vertGLM call.

Value

ds.vertOrdinal object: per-threshold fits + consolidated beta matrix + threshold-parameter vector.
